calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-01-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On May-22-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Declaration Of Keith M Velleca In Support Of Application For Court Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants-in-intervention Gibson Canites and Yeva, Inc., d/b/a Saxe Mortgage Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Keith M. Velleca, Esq. appeared with plaintiff-in-intervention Karen Lum, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defaulted defendants-in-intervention Gibson Canites or Yeva, Inc., d/b/a Saxe Mortgage Company.
Plaintiff's complaint-in-intervention was filed on January 12, 2017.
Defaults as to defendants-in-intervention Gibson Canites and Yeva, Inc., d/b/a Saxe Mortgage Company, were entered on February 22, 2018.
ROE defendants-in-intervention were dismissed without prejudice today, April 17, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff-in-Intervention Karen Lum, and against the Defaulted Defendants-in-Intervention Gibson Canites and Yeva, Inc., d/b/a Saxe Mortage Company, as follows:

Plaintiff-in-intervention Karen Lum shall be reimbursed the sum of $123,327.64 that she (Karen Lum) deposited with the Clerk of the San Francisco Superior Court on October 10, 2017 in connection with the underlying action entitled Gibson Canites vs. Yeva, Inc., d/b/a Saxe Mortgage Company, et al., San Francisco Superior Court Number CGC-17-561690. The Clerk of the San Francisco Superior Court shall issue and deliver a check payable to Karen Lum in the amount of $123,327.64, plus applicable interest.

Proposed Court Judgment on the complaint-in-intervention was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Continued To Apr-18-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For An Order Directing The Clerk To Release Cash Deposit
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: INTERVENOR KAREN LUM's Motion For An Order Directing The Clerk To Release Cash Deposit is OFF CALENDAR, premature. Complaint in intervention is not yet at issue. Additionally, the moving papers do not provide any authority for the proposition that the requested relief (which is also the relief prayed for in the Complaint in Intervention) may be sought by a motion. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Keith Velleca (no card provided) for Intervenor/Moving party KAREN LUM.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR KAREN LUM's Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention is GRANTED. No opposition filed. The proposed plaintiff in intervention has met her burden under Code of Civil Procedure section 387. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Arash Moussavian (415-695-4545) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Gibson Canites, specially appearing.
Nancy Ly (510-548-7474) for Defendant/Moving party Yeva, Inc.

The Court announce the tentative ruling. Defense counsel indicates Pltf's counsel did not give notice. Specially appearing counsel confimrs no notice was given. Specially appearing counsel makes an oral request on behlaf of Pltf's counsel to withdraw as counsel of record. The Court informs counsel a properly noticed motion will be required. No further argument. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YEVA, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant demurred to each cause of action. Plaintiff filed no opposition. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED.

Appearances: Arash Moussavan (415.695.4548) for plaintiff and moving party; Richard Beckman of Beckman Feller & Chang (415.871.0070) for defendant Yeva, Inc.

Plaintiff did not inform opposing counsel of its intent to argue. As both parties are present, the Court allows argument. Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to show any probability of prevailing on the merits on his claims alleged in the complaint. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
